Business Systems Readiness Assessment
Structured Question Framework – Draft 2 (Aligned to All Core
System Domains)
Purpose of this document:
To define a comprehensive but efficient question set that evaluates every core system domain outlined in
the Business Systems Pathways framework.
This version ensures we are assessing whether the business has repeatable systems that: - Capture leads -
Convert leads to clients - Deliver consistently - Retain and reactivate customers - Increase lifetime value -
Track performance - Leverage automation and AI appropriately
Estimated completion time: 10–12 minutes All answers are structured (no free text).
SECTION 1 — CLARITY & OFFER SYSTEM
Q1. How clearly defined is your primary offer?
A. I’m still refining what I sell.
B. I have multiple offers but no clear primary focus.
C. I have one clear primary offer and CTA.
D. I have one primary offer with defined positioning and proof (testimonials/results).
Rationale: - A → Foundation (systems cannot stabilize without clarity) - B → Foundation/Growth bridge - C
→ Growth readiness - D → Growth/Optimization readiness
SECTION 2 — PRESENCE SYSTEM
Q2. What best describes your online presence?
A. No structured website.
B. Basic website with limited conversion structure.
C. Structured website with clear conversion path.
D. Website + targeted landing pages aligned to offers.
Rationale: - A → Foundation - B → Foundation stable - C → Growth - D → Growth/Optimization
1

SECTION 3 — LEAD CAPTURE SYSTEM
Q3. How are leads captured?
A. Manual inquiries (email/DM).
B. Basic contact form or booking link.
C. Form/booking with automated confirmations.
D. Multi-step qualification (forms, conversational AI, funnels).
Rationale: - A → Foundation - B → Foundation - C → Growth - D → Growth/Optimization
SECTION 4 — CUSTOMER JOURNEY / CRM
SYSTEM
Q4. How structured is your CRM and customer journey?
A. Inbox or spreadsheet tracking.
B. CRM exists but inconsistent use.
C. Defined customer journey stages with consistent usage.
D. Lifecycle stages + segmentation + reporting.
Rationale: - A → Foundation - B → Foundation/Growth - C → Growth - D → Optimization readiness
SECTION 5 — NURTURE & FOLLOW-UP
SYSTEM
Q5. What follow-up happens after inquiry?
A. Manual replies only.
B. Templates but no automation.
C. Automated confirmations + reminders.
D. Multi-step nurture sequences tied to customer journey stages.
Rationale: - A → Foundation - B → Foundation - C → Growth - D → Growth/Optimization
2

SECTION 6 — DELIVERY SYSTEM
Q6. How standardized is client onboarding and fulfillment?
A. Varies each time.
B. Manual checklist but inconsistent.
C. Structured onboarding workflow.
D. Automated lifecycle transitions (onboarding → fulfillment → retention).
Rationale: - A → Foundation - B → Growth early - C → Growth mature - D → Optimization
SECTION 7 — PAYMENTS & OFFERS SYSTEM
Q7. How are payments and offers handled?
A. Manual invoicing only.
B. Online payments but limited automation.
C. Integrated checkout with confirmation workflows.
D. Subscriptions, order bumps, automated payment workflows.
Rationale: - A → Foundation - B → Growth early - C → Growth - D → Optimization
SECTION 8 — REACTIVATION & OUTREACH
SYSTEM
Q8. Do you actively monetize your existing database?
A. No reactivation efforts.
B. Occasional manual outreach.
C. Structured reactivation campaigns.
D. Automated lifecycle-based reactivation + Outreach AI.
Rationale: - A → Foundation/Growth - B → Growth - C → Growth/Optimization - D → Optimization
3

SECTION 9 — REPUTATION & REVIEWS SYSTEM
Q9. How do you generate and manage reviews?
A. No review system.
B. Manual requests occasionally.
C. Structured review request workflow.
D. Automated Reviews AI with follow-up and response workflows.
Rationale: - A → Foundation/Growth - B → Growth early - C → Growth - D → Optimization
SECTION 10 — AI ENGAGEMENT &
CONVERSION SYSTEM
Q10. How are AI tools used in your engagement process?
A. No AI tools in use.
B. AI tools installed but not integrated.
C. AI tools integrated into workflows (qualification, reminders).
D. AI voice/conversational agents driving engagement at scale.
Rationale: - A → Neutral baseline - B → Risk of premature optimization - C → Optimization readiness - D →
Optimization
SECTION 11 — REPORTING & IMPROVEMENT
SYSTEM
Q11. How do you track and improve performance?
A. I do not consistently track metrics.
B. I check results occasionally but no structured review.
C. I review dashboards regularly and adjust workflows.
D. I track lifecycle performance, attribution, and optimize continuously.
Rationale: - A → Foundation - B → Growth early - C → Growth - D → Optimization
4

SECTION 12 — LIFETIME VALUE &
RETENTION
Q12. How do you increase revenue from existing clients?
A. No structured retention strategy.
B. Occasional manual upsell offers.
C. Planned follow-up offers or renewal reminders.
D. Automated retention, renewal, and expansion workflows.
Rationale: - A → Foundation - B → Growth - C → Growth mature - D → Optimization
Classification Logic (Draft 2)
1. Any critical Foundation blockers (unclear offer, no CRM structure, no follow-up automation)
automatically prevent Optimization classification.
2. 3+ Foundation signals → Foundation Pathway.
3. Majority Growth signals with no blockers → Growth Pathway.
4. Optimization requires:
5. No Foundation blockers
6. At least 4 Optimization-level responses
7. Strong CRM + lifecycle + reporting maturity
Output Generation Structure
Once answers are submitted:
1. Aggregate rationales into:
2. Core strengths
3. Core gaps
4. Assign pathway based on lowest maturity constraints.
5. Generate:
6. 3–5 priority actions
7. 2–3 anti-priority warnings
8. Graduation outlook statement (what becomes possible next)
5

This Draft 2 now evaluates every core system domain in the Business Systems Pathways document
and ensures classification is based on full operational maturity, not surface-level sophistication.
6
